title: Strict TSConfig, TypeScript 7 Defaults, and Project Graphs · Independent Type Trial
summary: Create a clean multi-package configuration that fails on unsafe boundaries, unused work, and incompatible module assumptions. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a strict TypeScript 7 project configuration with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.
Account for TypeScript 7 strict, target, module, rootDir, types, and side-effect import defaults without legacy no-op flags.
Predict root files from files, include, exclude, imports, types, and library selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
